note: Thin library submodule registering the Google Charts backend for the charts module; adds a table chart type and loads Google's loader.js. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I want my Charts module charts to render with Google Charts. Which submodule do I enable and what library id does it register?
evals.expected_output: Enable charts_google; it registers the Chart library plugin id `google` (Google Charts), then select Google as the library.

evals.prompt: How is the Google Charts library loaded when using charts_google, and can I fully self-host it?
evals.expected_output: It loads Google's loader.js (google/charts v45 from gstatic.com); the actual chart code streams from Google's servers, so it is effectively always remote and cannot be fully self-hosted.

evals.prompt: Which chart type does the Google Charts submodule add on top of the base Charts chart types?
evals.expected_output: charts_google adds the `table` visualization type via charts_google.charts_types.yml.

evals.prompt: Does Google Charts support boxplot and candlestick charts through the Charts module?
evals.expected_output: Yes — google supports area, bar, boxplot, bubble, candlestick, column, donut, gauge, line, pie, scatter, spline, and table.

evals.prompt: In a Charts render array, how do I force a chart to use Google Charts?
evals.expected_output: Set '#chart_library' => 'google' on the chart element (or select Google in the Views style / block / field, or as the site default library).
